transcript.text: I think I spend way too much time on my phone

Let me see how much time you've spent on your phone today.No browser activity reports have been received yet. Connect the LifeOps browser activity source and try again.REGISTER_BROWSER_SESSION requires a deviceId.Specify a site domain.Autofill whitelist (47 entries): 1password.com, amazon.com, anthropic.com, apple.com, bankofamerica.com, bing.com, bitbucket.org, box.com, chase.com, citi.com, cloudflare.com, discord.com, dropbox.com, duckduckgo.com, ebay.com, facebook.com, figma.com, github.com, gitlab.com, gmail.com, google.com, googlemail.com, icloud.com, instagram.com, linear.app, linkedin.com, live.com, microsoft.com, netlify.com, notion.so, npmjs.com, office.com, openai.com, outlook.com, paypal.com, proton.me, protonmail.com, reddit.com, shopify.com, slack.com, stripe.com, twitter.com, vercel.com, wellsfargo.com, x.com, youtube.com, zoom.usNo browser activity has been reported yet.

llmCalls.prompt: {"text":"I think I spend way too much time on my phone"}

task: Generate dialog and actions for TestAgent.

context:
Possible response actions: IGNORE, GET_TIME_ON_SITE, X_READ, CALENDAR_ACTION, CALL_EXTERNAL, PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES, GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT, CHECK_AVAILABILITY, REQUEST_FIELD_FILL, SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS, RUN_MORNING_CHECKIN, LIST_ACTIVE_BLOCKS, REVOKE_REMOTE_SESSION, FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, START_REMOTE_SESSION, RELEASE_BLOCK, LIFE, LIST_REMOTE_SESSIONS, BLOCK_WEBSITES, MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER, NONE, UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, REMOTE_DESKTOP, HEALTH, REGISTER_BROWSER_SESSION, INBOX, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, MARK_FOLLOWUP_DONE, RELATIONSHIP, CALENDLY, UNBLOCK_APPS, GET_APP_BLOCK_STATUS, BLOCK_APPS, UPDATE_ENTITY, UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE, SCHEDULING, INTENT_SYNC, CALL_USER, REPLY, PASSWORD_MANAGER, SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD, RUN_NIGHT_CHECKIN, SUBSCRIPTIONS, GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS, BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE, GENERATE_DOSSIER, CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND, TWILIO_VOICE_CALL, LIST_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, ADD_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST, GET_TIME_ON_APP, DOSSIER, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, LIST_OVERDUE_FOLLOWUPS, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER

# Available Actions
actions[56]:
- GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT: T8d — Per-app time breakdown for the last N hours (default 24h). Returns noDataReason='macos-only' on non-Darwin platforms.
  aliases[3]: ACTIVITY_REPORT, WHAT_DID_I_WORK_ON, TIME_TRACKING_REPORT
  params[1]: windowHours?:number - Number of hours of history to report on (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "What did I work on today?" -> actions: GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT
- GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS: Admin-only. Check whether a local hosts-file website block is currently active and when it ends.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_GET_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_SELFCONTROL, IS_BLOCK_RUNNING
  example: User: "Is there a website block running right now?" -> actions: GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS
- UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES: Persist the owner's meeting scheduling preferences: preferred start/end of day (24h HH:MM local), blackout windows, default meeting duration, and travel buffer. These drive PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use this for durable sleep windows, no-call hours, and other recurring scheduling rules.
  aliases[7]: SET_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SAVE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SET_PREFERRED_TIMES, SET_BLACKOUT_WINDOWS, SLEEP_WINDOW, NO_CALL_HOURS, PROTECT_SLEEP
  params[6]: timeZone?:string - IANA time zone used to interpret preferred hours.; preferredStartLocal?:string - Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; preferredEndLocal?:string - Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; defaultDurationMinutes?:number - Default meeting duration in minutes (5–480).; travelBufferMinutes?:number - Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).; blackoutWindows?:array - Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.
  example: User: "No calls between 11pm and 8am unless I explicitly say it's okay." -> actions: UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES
- IGNORE: Call this action if ignoring the user. If the user is aggressive, creepy or is finished with the conversation, use this action. Or, if both you and the user have already said goodbye, use this action instead of saying bye again. Use IGNORE any time the conversation has naturally ended. Do not use IGNORE if the user has engaged directly, or if something went wrong and you need to tell them. Only ignore if the user should be ignored.
  aliases[3]: STOP_TALKING, STOP_CHATTING, STOP_CONVERSATION
  example: User: "Go screw yourself" -> actions: IGNORE
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY
  params[5]: action?:string [values=create|create_goal|update|update_goal|delete|delete_goal|complete|skip|snooze|review|phone|escalation|reminder_preference|calendar|next_event|email|overview] - What kind of life operation to perform.; intent?:string - Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. May include: cadence (schedule object), kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), minutes (snooze minutes), phoneNumber, allowSms, allowVoice, steps (escalation steps array), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops), or reminder preference targeting.
  example: User: "add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow" -> actions: LIFE
- TWILIO_VOICE_CALL: Place a voice call via Twilio. Use only for urgent escalations or explicit voice-call requests from the owner. Use this for real phone calls, not for calendar-only rescheduling or advice. Always drafts first; the caller must pass confirmed: true to actually dial.
  aliases[6]: CALL_ME, PLACE_CALL, VOICE_CALL, TWILIO_CALL, CALL_DENTIST, PHONE_SUPPORT
  params[3]: to:string - Destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).; message:string - Plaintext message to speak via TwiML. Will be XML-escaped before dispatch.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the owner has explicitly confirmed the call should be placed.
  example: User: "Call me at +15551234567 and say the build is done" -> actions: TWILIO_VOICE_CALL
- REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION: Admin-only. Prepare local website blocking by requesting administrator/root approval when the machine supports it, or explain the manual change needed when it does not.
  aliases[5]: ENABLE_WEBSITE_BLOCKING, ALLOW_WEBSITE_BLOCKING, GRANT_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, REQUEST_SELFCONTROL_PERMISSION
  example: User: "Give yourself permission to block websites on this machine." -> actions: REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION
- MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER: Read and control the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. This is not Milady Desktop Browser.
  aliases[3]: PERSONAL_BROWSER, LIFEOPS_BROWSER, MANAGE_PERSONAL_BROWSER
  params[6]: command?:string - Browser command: get_settings, update_settings, list_companions, list_tabs, read_current_page, list_sessions, get_session, confirm_session, complete_session, or a browser control command.; url?:string - Target URL for open or navigate commands.; selector?:string - DOM selector for click, type, or submit commands.; text?:string - Input text for type commands.; sessionId?:string - Browser session id for get_session, confirm_session, or complete_session.; settings?:object - Settings payload for update_settings.
- REQUEST_FIELD_FILL: Ask the LifeOps browser extension to autofill a field via the installed password manager (1Password or ProtonPass). Refuses on domains not in the user's autofill whitelist. Credentials never pass through the agent — the extension resolves them locally.
  aliases[3]: AUTOFILL_FIELD, AUTOFILL_REQUEST, FILL_PASSWORD_FIELD
  params[4]: tabUrl?:string - URL of the tab where the field should be filled. Used for whitelist enforcement.; fieldPurpose?:string - One of: email, password, name, phone, custom. Tells the password manager which field to resolve.; fieldSelector?:string - Optional CSS selector narrowing which field to fill on the page.; customKey?:string - When fieldPurpose is 'custom', the key in the password-manager item to resolve (e.g. 'API key').
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.
  aliases[4]: CALENDLY_LIST_EVENT_TYPES, CALENDLY_AVAILABILITY, CALENDLY_UPCOMING, CALENDLY_BOOKING_LINK
  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.
  example: User: "Show me my Calendly event types" -> actions: CALENDLY
- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- SCHEDULING: Multi-turn scheduling coordinator. Manages negotiations with another party across proposal/response rounds: start a negotiation, propose times, record responses, finalize the confirmed slot, or cancel.
  aliases[4]: SCHEDULE_MEETING, NEGOTIATE_MEETING, COORDINATE_SCHEDULE, MULTI_TURN_SCHEDULING
  params[10]: subaction?:string - Which step of the negotiation to run: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, list_active, list_proposals.; intent?:string - Free-text description of what the scheduling turn is trying to do.; negotiationId?:string - Target negotiation ID for proposal, finalize, cancel, or list_proposals.; proposalId?:string - Target proposal ID for respond or finalize.; subject?:string - Subject of the meeting (used when starting a negotiation).; startAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed start time.; endAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed end time.; durationMinutes?:number - Meeting duration in minutes (defaults to 30 when starting).; response?:string - Proposal response: accepted, declined, or expired.; confirmed?:boolean - Set true alongside a proposalId to finalize.
  example: User: "Start a scheduling negotiation with Alice about the quarterly review" -> actions: SCHEDULING
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER
- GET_APP_BLOCK_STATUS: Admin-only. Check whether an app block is currently active and when it ends.
  aliases[3]: CHECK_APP_BLOCK_STATUS, IS_APP_BLOCK_RUNNING, APP_BLOCK_STATUS
  example: User: "Is there an app block running?" -> actions: GET_APP_BLOCK_STATUS
- UNBLOCK_APPS: Admin-only. Remove the current app block, unshielding all blocked apps.
  aliases[4]: UNBLOCK_APP, REMOVE_APP_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_APPS, UNSHIELD_APPS
  example: User: "Unblock my apps." -> actions: UNBLOCK_APPS
- CALL_EXTERNAL: Place an outbound phone call to a third party via Twilio. Use this for approved booking, reschedule, outage, support, or escalation calls to vendors or counterparties. Examples: 'call the dentist and reschedule my appointment', 'phone my cable company and ask about the outage', 'call the airline', or 'call the hotel to rebook'. This action can draft the call, ask which saved contact to use, and then require confirmation before dialing. If the user wants a real phone call to a third party, prefer this action over CALENDAR_ACTION, LIFE, or CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND. The recipient must appear in the configured allow-list before the actual call is placed.
  aliases[9]: PHONE_EXTERNAL, DIAL_EXTERNAL, CALL_THIRD_PARTY, BOOK_BY_PHONE, REBOOK_BY_PHONE, CALL_DENTIST, PHONE_CABLE_COMPANY, CALL_SUPPORT, RESCHEDULE_APPOINTMENT_BY_PHONE
  params[3]: confirmed?:boolean - Must be true to actually place the call.; to?:string - E.164 phone number to call. Must appear in the external allow-list.; message?:string - Spoken message played when the recipient answers.
  example: User: "If needed, call the airline and help rebook the other thing." -> actions: CALL_EXTERNAL
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES
- RELATIONSHIP: Manage contacts, relationships, and follow-ups. Subactions: list_contacts, add_contact, log_interaction, add_follow_up, complete_follow_up, follow_up_list, days_since.
  aliases[5]: CONTACT, ROLODEX, FOLLOW_UP, LOG_INTERACTION, ADD_CONTACT
  params[13]: subaction?:string - Which relationship operation to run: list_contacts, add_contact, log_interaction, add_follow_up, complete_follow_up, follow_up_list, days_since.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not set.; name?:string - Contact display name.; channel?:string - Primary channel for the contact (email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp, x_dm).; handle?:string - Primary handle/address on the chosen channel.; email?:string - Optional email address for the contact.; phone?:string - Optional phone number for the contact.; notes?:string - Free-form notes or interaction summary.; relationshipId?:string - Target relationship id.; followUpId?:string - Target follow-up id.; reason?:string - Reason or purpose for a follow-up.; dueAt?:string - ISO-8601 due time for a follow-up.; confirmed?:boolean - Optional explicit confirmation flag.
  example: User: "Show me my contacts." -> actions: RELATIONSHIP
- RUN_NIGHT_CHECKIN: Run the night check-in: review today's meetings, completed wins, and any overdue todos for the owner. Use this for a dedicated evening wrap-up, not for inbox daily briefs, unread cross-channel summaries, or reply workflows.
  aliases[3]: NIGHT_CHECKIN, START_NIGHT_CHECKIN, EVENING_CHECKIN
- GENERATE_DOSSIER: Low-level event-specific dossier generation for an already identified calendar event. Use this only when the event id or fuzzy event title is already known from a prior workflow. Prefer DOSSIER for generic user requests like 'brief me', 'background on the person I'm meeting', or 'prep me for my next meeting'.
  aliases[3]: MEETING_DOSSIER_BY_EVENT, EVENT_DOSSIER, GENERATE_DOSSIER_FROM_EVENT
  params[3]: eventId?:string - Calendar event id. Mutually optional with eventTitleFuzzy.; eventTitleFuzzy?:string - Fuzzy title match used when eventId is unknown (e.g. 'my 3pm').; windowDays?:number - Context window in days (default 7).
  example: User: "Generate a dossier for event 'Board sync tomorrow'" -> actions: GENERATE_DOSSIER
- GET_TIME_ON_APP: T8d — Time spent on a specific app (matched by app name or bundle id) over the last N hours.
  aliases[2]: TIME_IN_APP, HOW_LONG_IN_APP
  params[2]: appNameOrBundleId?:string - App name (e.g. 'Safari') or bundle id (e.g. 'com.apple.Safari').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I in VS Code today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_APP
- X_READ: Read X/Twitter DMs, the home timeline or mentions feed, or run a recent search. Use this for requests like 'check my Twitter DMs', 'what's on my X timeline?', 'show me my mentions', or 'search Twitter for posts about elizaOS'. Use this for retrieving content from X, not posting. Do not reply that X/Twitter access is unavailable when this action is registered and visible.
  aliases[10]: READ_X, READ_TWITTER, CHECK_TWITTER_DMS, TWITTER_TIMELINE, X_TIMELINE, TWITTER_MENTIONS, SEARCH_TWITTER, X_DMS, X_FEED, X_SEARCH
  params[5]: subaction?:string [values=read_dms|read_feed|search] - X read operation. read_dms for direct messages, read_feed for timeline/mentions, search for recent tweet search.; intent?:string - Free-text description of the request, e.g. "check my X DMs", "show my mentions", "search X for elizaOS".; query?:string - Search query string for the search subaction.; feedType?:string [values=home_timeline|mentions|search] - Feed to read for read_feed. One of home_timeline (default), mentions, or search.; limit?:number - Max items to return (1-100).
  example: User: "Check my X DMs." -> actions: X_READ
- GET_TIME_ON_SITE: T8d — Time on a specific site based on browser activity reports pushed into the runtime store.
  aliases[2]: TIME_ON_WEBSITE, TIME_ON_DOMAIN
  params[2]: domain?:string - Hostname (e.g. 'github.com').; windowHours?:number - Window in hours (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "How long was I on github.com today?" -> actions: GET_TIME_ON_SITE
- SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD: Set the follow-up threshold (in days) for a specific contact. Requires a positive integer threshold and either contactId or an unambiguous contactName.
  aliases[3]: FOLLOWUP_RULE, CHANGE_FOLLOWUP_INTERVAL, SET_CONTACT_FREQUENCY_DAYS
  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; thresholdDays:number - Number of days after last contact before this contact is considered overdue.
  example: User: "Remind me to follow up with Dana every 14 days" -> actions: SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD
- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Use search_events for flights, itinerary, travel, appointments, or keyword lookup; feed for agenda/schedule reads; next_event for the next upcoming event; create_event only when creating a new event.; intent?:string - Natural language calendar request, especially schedule or itinerary questions. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owner only.
  aliases[14]: CHECK_INBOX, INBOX_TRIAGE, INBOX_DIGEST, INBOX_RESPOND, TRIAGE_INBOX, SCAN_MESSAGES, CHECK_MESSAGES, DAILY_DIGEST, DAILY_BRIEF, INBOX_SUMMARY, REPLY_INBOX, RESPOND_TO_MESSAGE, MISSED_CALL_FOLLOWUP, GROUP_CHAT_HANDOFF
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=triage|digest|respond] - Inbox operation to run: triage (scan channels for new messages), digest (daily summary), or respond (draft/send a reply).; intent?:string - Natural language inbox request. Examples: "check my inbox for new messages", "give me my daily summary", "respond to Alice's Discord message".; target?:string - Who to respond to (for respond subaction). Can be a sender name, channel name, or source platform.; entryId?:string - Specific triage entry ID to respond to.; messageText?:string - Pre-composed message text for the response.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the user is confirming a previously drafted response.
  example: User: "Triage my inbox" -> actions: INBOX
- ADD_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST: Add a domain to the autofill whitelist. Requires explicit user confirmation (confirmed: true). Persisted to the local profile store.
  aliases[2]: TRUST_SITE_FOR_AUTOFILL, APPROVE_AUTOFILL_DOMAIN
  params[2]: domain?:string - Domain to add. Stored as a registrable domain (e.g. 'example.com'). Subdomains are covered by the parent entry.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true. Required to ensure the user approved the addition, not the agent.
- DOSSIER: Generate a pre-meeting or person-background briefing dossier with context about attendees, recent interactions, and upcoming event details. Use this for requests like 'pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella', 'give me the background on the person I'm meeting next: Julia Chen', or 'brief me for my next meeting'. If the user explicitly wants a brief, backgrounder, prep sheet, or dossier, use this action instead of replying from ENTITIES, FACTS, or memory alone.
  aliases[9]: GENERATE_DOSSIER, PREMEETING_BRIEFING, MEETING_BRIEFING, PREPARE_FOR_MEETING, BRIEF_ME, BACKGROUND_BRIEF, PERSON_BACKGROUND, NEXT_MEETING_BRIEF, WHO_AM_I_MEETING
  params[5]: intent?:string - Natural language request. Examples: "brief me for my 2pm with Alice", "prep me for tomorrow's board meeting".; calendarEventId?:string - Optional calendar event id to pull event details (title, time, location, attendees).; subject?:string - Subject line for the dossier (e.g. meeting title or topic).; attendeeHandles?:array of string - List of attendee handles/emails to look up in the relationships table.; generatedForAt?:string - ISO timestamp the dossier is generated for (defaults to now).
  example: User: "Pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella" -> actions: DOSSIER
- RELEASE_BLOCK: Release an active website block rule. Requires confirmed:true. harsh_no_bypass rules cannot be released via confirmation — they must wait for gate fulfillment.
  aliases[3]: RELEASE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, END_BLOCK_RULE, BYPASS_BLOCK_RULE

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

rules[19]:
- think briefly, then respond
- always include a <thought> field, even for direct replies
- actions execute in listed order
- if replying without another grounded state/action query, REPLY goes first
- use IGNORE or STOP only by themselves
- include providers only when needed
- when the user asks about uploaded files, documents, prior uploads, or knowledge-base contents, call the relevant providers before replying instead of asking the user to resend the material
- use provider_hints from context when present instead of restating the same rules
- if an action needs inputs, include them inside that action's <params> block
- if a required param is unknown, ask for clarification in text
- for live status questions or remaining-work queries, do not answer from recent conversation alone; call the relevant action/provider to refresh state, and do not pair it with a speculative REPLY that guesses the result
- when an action will fetch the state and produce the final grounded answer, do not add REPLY just to say "checking", "let me look", or similar filler; use the action alone and leave text empty
- when the user asks you to create, store, remember, schedule, remind, upload, follow up, route, escalate, or set a standing policy, choose the matching action instead of handling it in prose only
- for standing or future-condition requests like "if/when X, do Y", still choose the action that records, queues, or routes that behavior on the first turn
- if a matching action can own the task and ask the missing follow-up itself, still select that action and put the clarification in text; do not reply in prose alone
- when the user defines a durable preference, recurring block, escalation policy, upload policy, approval-gated workflow, or multi-device reminder rule, select the owning action even if some implementation details are still missing
- do not wait for portal names, priority labels, event IDs, exact travel preferences, or the definition of "important" before selecting the owning action; let the action gather those details
- for LifeOps create requests with a clear defaultable habit or natural window, such as drinking water, stretch breaks during the day, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, or brushing when waking up and before bed, call LIFE instead of asking for exact clock times unless the user explicitly asks for precise scheduling
- only choose actions that directly satisfy the user's request or an explicit live-state question; do not opportunistically triage inboxes, summarize calendars, propose meetings, or call adjacent tools just because provider context makes them available
- when the user is venting, reflecting, stating an opinion, or asking for generic advice about a domain, stay in REPLY or NONE unless they explicitly ask you to inspect state, change state, send something, schedule something, or perform a real operation

control_actions:
- STOP means the task is done and the agent should end the run without executing more actions
- STOP is a terminal control action even if it is not listed in available actions

fields[5]{name,meaning}:
- thought | short plan
- actions | ordered <action> entries inside <actions>
- providers | array of provider names, or empty
- text | next message for TestAgent
- simple | true or false

formatting:
- wrap multi-line code in fenced code blocks
- use inline backticks for short code identifiers
<output>
Return only XML. No prose before or after it. No <think>.




Use this shape:
<response>
  <thought>Your internal reasoning about the message and what to do</thought>
  <actions>Ordered action entries. For XML, use one or more <action><name>ACTION_NAME</name><params>...</params></action> blocks inside <actions>.</actions>
  <providers>Optional provider names to call before the final reply or action. Use an empty field when no provider lookup is needed.</providers>
  <text>The text response to send to the user</text>
  <simple>Whether this is a simple response (true/false)</simple>
</response>


Return exactly one <response>...</response>.
</output>

{"action":"send_message"|"send_email"|"schedule_event"|"modify_event"|"cancel_event"|"book_travel"|"make_call"|"execute_workflow"|"spend_money"|"noop",
"channel":"telegram"|"discord"|"slack"|"imessage"|"sms"|"email"|"google_calendar"|"browser"|"phone"|"internal",
"requiresApproval":true|false,
"reason":"short justification",
"payload":{...action-specific fields, or empty object when action=noop}}

Rules:
- Choose action=noop when no action is warranted this tick (e.g. nothing overdue).
- requiresApproval=true for any action that touches a person or external system.
- requiresApproval=false ONLY when action=noop or the action is purely internal (logging, internal workflow with no side effects).
- The reason must explain WHY this tick warrants the chosen action, in any language.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

- GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT: T8d — Per-app time breakdown for the last N hours (default 24h). Returns noDataReason='macos-only' on non-Darwin platforms.
  aliases[3]: ACTIVITY_REPORT, WHAT_DID_I_WORK_ON, TIME_TRACKING_REPORT
  params[1]: windowHours?:number - Number of hours of history to report on (default 24, max 720).
  example: User: "What did I work on today?" -> actions: GET_ACTIVITY_REPORT
- GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS: Admin-only. Check whether a local hosts-file website block is currently active and when it ends.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_GET_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS, CHECK_SELFCONTROL, IS_BLOCK_RUNNING
  example: User: "Is there a website block running right now?" -> actions: GET_WEBSITE_BLOCK_STATUS
- UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES: Persist the owner's meeting scheduling preferences: preferred start/end of day (24h HH:MM local), blackout windows, default meeting duration, and travel buffer. These drive PROPOSE_MEETING_TIMES. Use this for durable sleep windows, no-call hours, and other recurring scheduling rules.
  aliases[7]: SET_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SAVE_MEETING_PREFERENCES, SET_PREFERRED_TIMES, SET_BLACKOUT_WINDOWS, SLEEP_WINDOW, NO_CALL_HOURS, PROTECT_SLEEP
  params[6]: timeZone?:string - IANA time zone used to interpret preferred hours.; preferredStartLocal?:string - Earliest preferred meeting start time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; preferredEndLocal?:string - Latest preferred meeting end time-of-day (local HH:MM, 24h).; defaultDurationMinutes?:number - Default meeting duration in minutes (5–480).; travelBufferMinutes?:number - Minutes to reserve before/after each meeting (0–240).; blackoutWindows?:array - Array of { label, startLocal (HH:MM), endLocal (HH:MM), daysOfWeek? (0=Sun..6=Sat) }.
  example: User: "No calls between 11pm and 8am unless I explicitly say it's okay." -> actions: UPDATE_MEETING_PREFERENCES
- IGNORE: Call this action if ignoring the user. If the user is aggressive, creepy or is finished with the conversation, use this action. Or, if both you and the user have already said goodbye, use this action instead of saying bye again. Use IGNORE any time the conversation has naturally ended. Do not use IGNORE if the user has engaged directly, or if something went wrong and you need to tell them. Only ignore if the user should be ignored.
  aliases[3]: STOP_TALKING, STOP_CHATTING, STOP_CONVERSATION
  example: User: "Go screw yourself" -> actions: IGNORE
- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY
  params[5]: action?:string [values=create|create_goal|update|update_goal|delete|delete_goal|complete|skip|snooze|review|phone|escalation|reminder_preference|calendar|next_event|email|overview] - What kind of life operation to perform.; intent?:string - Natural language description of what to do. Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. May include: cadence (schedule object), kind (task/habit/routine), description, priority, progressionRule, reminderPlan, confirmed (boolean when the user explicitly approves a previewed create), preset (snooze preset like 15m/30m/1h/tonight/tomorrow_morning), minutes (snooze minutes), phoneNumber, allowSms, allowVoice, steps (escalation steps array), goalId, goalTitle, supportStrategy, successCriteria, note, limit, domain (user_lifeops/agent_ops), or reminder preference targeting.
  example: User: "add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow" -> actions: LIFE
- TWILIO_VOICE_CALL: Place a voice call via Twilio. Use only for urgent escalations or explicit voice-call requests from the owner. Use this for real phone calls, not for calendar-only rescheduling or advice. Always drafts first; the caller must pass confirmed: true to actually dial.
  aliases[6]: CALL_ME, PLACE_CALL, VOICE_CALL, TWILIO_CALL, CALL_DENTIST, PHONE_SUPPORT
  params[3]: to:string - Destination phone number in E.164 format (e.g. +15551234567).; message:string - Plaintext message to speak via TwiML. Will be XML-escaped before dispatch.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the owner has explicitly confirmed the call should be placed.
  example: User: "Call me at +15551234567 and say the build is done" -> actions: TWILIO_VOICE_CALL
- REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION: Admin-only. Prepare local website blocking by requesting administrator/root approval when the machine supports it, or explain the manual change needed when it does not.
  aliases[5]: ENABLE_WEBSITE_BLOCKING, ALLOW_WEBSITE_BLOCKING, GRANT_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION, REQUEST_SELFCONTROL_PERMISSION
  example: User: "Give yourself permission to block websites on this machine." -> actions: REQUEST_WEBSITE_BLOCKING_PERMISSION
- MANAGE_LIFEOPS_BROWSER: Read and control the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. This is not Milady Desktop Browser.
  aliases[3]: PERSONAL_BROWSER, LIFEOPS_BROWSER, MANAGE_PERSONAL_BROWSER
  params[6]: command?:string - Browser command: get_settings, update_settings, list_companions, list_tabs, read_current_page, list_sessions, get_session, confirm_session, complete_session, or a browser control command.; url?:string - Target URL for open or navigate commands.; selector?:string - DOM selector for click, type, or submit commands.; text?:string - Input text for type commands.; sessionId?:string - Browser session id for get_session, confirm_session, or complete_session.; settings?:object - Settings payload for update_settings.
- REQUEST_FIELD_FILL: Ask the LifeOps browser extension to autofill a field via the installed password manager (1Password or ProtonPass). Refuses on domains not in the user's autofill whitelist. Credentials never pass through the agent — the extension resolves them locally.
  aliases[3]: AUTOFILL_FIELD, AUTOFILL_REQUEST, FILL_PASSWORD_FIELD
  params[4]: tabUrl?:string - URL of the tab where the field should be filled. Used for whitelist enforcement.; fieldPurpose?:string - One of: email, password, name, phone, custom. Tells the password manager which field to resolve.; fieldSelector?:string - Optional CSS selector narrowing which field to fill on the page.; customKey?:string - When fieldPurpose is 'custom', the key in the password-manager item to resolve (e.g. 'API key').
- FETCH_BROWSER_ACTIVITY: Return the most recent per-domain focus time pushed by the LifeOps browser extension. Parameters: deviceId (optional), limit (optional, default 10).
  aliases[3]: BROWSER_ACTIVITY, GET_BROWSER_ACTIVITY, TIME_ON_SITE
- CALENDLY: Work with Calendly: list event types, check availability, list scheduled events, generate single-use booking links. Subactions: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.

  params[6]: subaction:string - One of: list_event_types, availability, upcoming_events, single_use_link.; intent?:string - Optional free-form description of the user's intent.; eventTypeUri?:string - Calendly event type URI. Required for availability and single_use_link.; startDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries (availability, upcoming_events).; endDate?:string - ISO date (YYYY-MM-DD) for range-based queries.; timezone?:string - IANA timezone, e.g. America/Los_Angeles.

- REPLY: Replies to the current conversation with the text from the generated message. Default if the agent is responding with a message and no other action. Use REPLY at the beginning of a chain of actions as an acknowledgement, and at the end of a chain of actions as a final response.
  aliases[5]: GREET, REPLY_TO_MESSAGE, SEND_REPLY, RESPOND, RESPONSE
  example: User: "Hello there!" -> actions: REPLY
- SCHEDULING: Multi-turn scheduling coordinator. Manages negotiations with another party across proposal/response rounds: start a negotiation, propose times, record responses, finalize the confirmed slot, or cancel.
  aliases[4]: SCHEDULE_MEETING, NEGOTIATE_MEETING, COORDINATE_SCHEDULE, MULTI_TURN_SCHEDULING
  params[10]: subaction?:string - Which step of the negotiation to run: start, propose, respond, finalize, cancel, list_active, list_proposals.; intent?:string - Free-text description of what the scheduling turn is trying to do.; negotiationId?:string - Target negotiation ID for proposal, finalize, cancel, or list_proposals.; proposalId?:string - Target proposal ID for respond or finalize.; subject?:string - Subject of the meeting (used when starting a negotiation).; startAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed start time.; endAt?:string - ISO-8601 proposed end time.; durationMinutes?:number - Meeting duration in minutes (defaults to 30 when starting).; response?:string - Proposal response: accepted, declined, or expired.; confirmed?:boolean - Set true alongside a proposalId to finalize.
  example: User: "Start a scheduling negotiation with Alice about the quarterly review" -> actions: SCHEDULING
- COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER: Compute a travel-time buffer (in minutes) for an upcoming calendar event using Google Maps Distance Matrix. Returns an explicit fallback result when GOOGLE_MAPS_API_KEY is absent or the call fails.
  aliases[4]: TRAVEL_TIME, COMPUTE_TRAVEL_TIME, BLOCK_TRAVEL_TIME, ESTIMATE_TRAVEL
  params[2]: eventId:string - Calendar event id whose location is the destination.; originAddress?:string - Optional starting address. When omitted, the service attempts to use a default origin or falls back to a fixed buffer.
  example: User: "How much travel time should I block for evt-42?" -> actions: COMPUTE_TRAVEL_BUFFER

- CALL_EXTERNAL: Place an outbound phone call to a third party via Twilio. Use this for approved booking, reschedule, outage, support, or escalation calls to vendors or counterparties. Examples: 'call the dentist and reschedule my appointment', 'phone my cable company and ask about the outage', 'call the airline', or 'call the hotel to rebook'. This action can draft the call, ask which saved contact to use, and then require confirmation before dialing. If the user wants a real phone call to a third party, prefer this action over CALENDAR_ACTION, LIFE, or CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND. The recipient must appear in the configured allow-list before the actual call is placed.
  aliases[9]: PHONE_EXTERNAL, DIAL_EXTERNAL, CALL_THIRD_PARTY, BOOK_BY_PHONE, REBOOK_BY_PHONE, CALL_DENTIST, PHONE_CABLE_COMPANY, CALL_SUPPORT, RESCHEDULE_APPOINTMENT_BY_PHONE
  params[3]: confirmed?:boolean - Must be true to actually place the call.; to?:string - E.164 phone number to call. Must appear in the external allow-list.; message?:string - Spoken message played when the recipient answers.
  example: User: "If needed, call the airline and help rebook the other thing." -> actions: CALL_EXTERNAL
- UNBLOCK_WEBSITES: Admin-only. Remove the current local website block by restoring the system hosts file entries Eliza added.
  aliases[4]: SELFCONTROL_UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, UNBLOCK_WEBSITES, REMOVE_WEBSITE_BLOCK, STOP_BLOCKING_SITES
  example: User: "Unblock x.com right now." -> actions: UNBLOCK_WEBSITES

  aliases[5]: CONTACT, ROLODEX, FOLLOW_UP, LOG_INTERACTION, ADD_CONTACT
  params[13]: subaction?:string - Which relationship operation to run: list_contacts, add_contact, log_interaction, add_follow_up, complete_follow_up, follow_up_list, days_since.; intent?:string - Free-form user intent used to infer subaction when not set.; name?:string - Contact display name.; channel?:string - Primary channel for the contact (email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp, x_dm).; handle?:string - Primary handle/address on the chosen channel.; email?:string - Optional email address for the contact.; phone?:string - Optional phone number for the contact.; notes?:string - Free-form notes or interaction summary.; relationshipId?:string - Target relationship id.; followUpId?:string - Target follow-up id.; reason?:string - Reason or purpose for a follow-up.; dueAt?:string - ISO-8601 due time for a follow-up.; confirmed?:boolean - Optional explicit confirmation flag.

- RUN_NIGHT_CHECKIN: Run the night check-in: review today's meetings, completed wins, and any overdue todos for the owner. Use this for a dedicated evening wrap-up, not for inbox daily briefs, unread cross-channel summaries, or reply workflows.
  aliases[3]: NIGHT_CHECKIN, START_NIGHT_CHECKIN, EVENING_CHECKIN
- GENERATE_DOSSIER: Low-level event-specific dossier generation for an already identified calendar event. Use this only when the event id or fuzzy event title is already known from a prior workflow. Prefer DOSSIER for generic user requests like 'brief me', 'background on the person I'm meeting', or 'prep me for my next meeting'.
  aliases[3]: MEETING_DOSSIER_BY_EVENT, EVENT_DOSSIER, GENERATE_DOSSIER_FROM_EVENT
  params[3]: eventId?:string - Calendar event id. Mutually optional with eventTitleFuzzy.; eventTitleFuzzy?:string - Fuzzy title match used when eventId is unknown (e.g. 'my 3pm').; windowDays?:number - Context window in days (default 7).
  example: User: "Generate a dossier for event 'Board sync tomorrow'" -> actions: GENERATE_DOSSIER

- X_READ: Read X/Twitter DMs, the home timeline or mentions feed, or run a recent search. Use this for requests like 'check my Twitter DMs', 'what's on my X timeline?', 'show me my mentions', or 'search Twitter for posts about elizaOS'. Use this for retrieving content from X, not posting. Do not reply that X/Twitter access is unavailable when this action is registered and visible.

  params[5]: subaction?:string [values=read_dms|read_feed|search] - X read operation. read_dms for direct messages, read_feed for timeline/mentions, search for recent tweet search.; intent?:string - Free-text description of the request, e.g. "check my X DMs", "show my mentions", "search X for elizaOS".; query?:string - Search query string for the search subaction.; feedType?:string [values=home_timeline|mentions|search] - Feed to read for read_feed. One of home_timeline (default), mentions, or search.; limit?:number - Max items to return (1-100).

- SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD: Set the follow-up threshold (in days) for a specific contact. Requires a positive integer threshold and either contactId or an unambiguous contactName.
  aliases[3]: FOLLOWUP_RULE, CHANGE_FOLLOWUP_INTERVAL, SET_CONTACT_FREQUENCY_DAYS
  params[3]: contactId?:string - UUID of the contact. Preferred when known.; contactName?:string - Human-readable contact name. Must be unambiguous across stored contacts.; thresholdDays:number - Number of days after last contact before this contact is considered overdue.
  example: User: "Remind me to follow up with Dana every 14 days" -> actions: SET_FOLLOWUP_THRESHOLD
- CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action.
  aliases[17]: CALENDAR, CHECK_CALENDAR, SHOW_CALENDAR_TODAY, TODAY_SCHEDULE, WEEK_AHEAD, WEEK_VIEW, WHATS_MY_NEXT_MEETING, SCHEDULE_EVENT, CREATE_CALENDAR_EVENT, SEARCH_CALENDAR, NEXT_MEETING, ITINERARY, TRAVEL_SCHEDULE, CHECK_SCHEDULE, BOOK_TIME_BLOCK, RECURRING_TIME_BLOCK, REBOOK_TRAVEL
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=feed|next_event|search_events|create_event|update_event|delete_event|trip_window] - Calendar operation. Use search_events for flights, itinerary, travel, appointments, or keyword lookup; feed for agenda/schedule reads; next_event for the next upcoming event; create_event only when creating a new event.; intent?:string - Natural language calendar request, especially schedule or itinerary questions. Examples: "what is on my calendar today", "do i have any flights this week", "when do i fly back from denver", "create a meeting tomorrow at 3pm".; title?:string - Event title when creating an event. Optional for read/search actions.; query?:string - Short search phrase for search_events, such as flight, dentist, Denver, or return flight.; queries?:array of string - Optional array of search phrases for search_events. The action will combine and dedupe them.; details?:object - Optional structured calendar fields such as time bounds, timezone, calendar id, create-event timing, location, and attendees.
  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION
- INBOX: Unified inbox management: triage new messages across all channels, generate a daily digest summary, or draft/send a response to a triaged item. Use this for executive-assistant daily briefs, urgent-vs-low priority inbox ranking, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, missed-call repair follow-up, and group-chat handoff coordination. Examples: 'triage my inbox', 'give me my inbox digest', or 'respond to the messages that need an answer in my inbox'. DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow. If the request is explicitly Gmail or email-specific, about unread emails, or about drafting or sending a reply to a specific email, use GMAIL_ACTION instead. Subactions: triage, digest, respond. Admin/owner only.
  aliases[14]: CHECK_INBOX, INBOX_TRIAGE, INBOX_DIGEST, INBOX_RESPOND, TRIAGE_INBOX, SCAN_MESSAGES, CHECK_MESSAGES, DAILY_DIGEST, DAILY_BRIEF, INBOX_SUMMARY, REPLY_INBOX, RESPOND_TO_MESSAGE, MISSED_CALL_FOLLOWUP, GROUP_CHAT_HANDOFF
  params[6]: subaction?:string [values=triage|digest|respond] - Inbox operation to run: triage (scan channels for new messages), digest (daily summary), or respond (draft/send a reply).; intent?:string - Natural language inbox request. Examples: "check my inbox for new messages", "give me my daily summary", "respond to Alice's Discord message".; target?:string - Who to respond to (for respond subaction). Can be a sender name, channel name, or source platform.; entryId?:string - Specific triage entry ID to respond to.; messageText?:string - Pre-composed message text for the response.; confirmed?:boolean - Set to true when the user is confirming a previously drafted response.
  example: User: "Triage my inbox" -> actions: INBOX
- ADD_AUTOFILL_WHITELIST: Add a domain to the autofill whitelist. Requires explicit user confirmation (confirmed: true). Persisted to the local profile store.
  aliases[2]: TRUST_SITE_FOR_AUTOFILL, APPROVE_AUTOFILL_DOMAIN
  params[2]: domain?:string - Domain to add. Stored as a registrable domain (e.g. 'example.com'). Subdomains are covered by the parent entry.; confirmed?:boolean - Must be explicitly true. Required to ensure the user approved the addition, not the agent.
- DOSSIER: Generate a pre-meeting or person-background briefing dossier with context about attendees, recent interactions, and upcoming event details. Use this for requests like 'pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella', 'give me the background on the person I'm meeting next: Julia Chen', or 'brief me for my next meeting'. If the user explicitly wants a brief, backgrounder, prep sheet, or dossier, use this action instead of replying from ENTITIES, FACTS, or memory alone.
  aliases[9]: GENERATE_DOSSIER, PREMEETING_BRIEFING, MEETING_BRIEFING, PREPARE_FOR_MEETING, BRIEF_ME, BACKGROUND_BRIEF, PERSON_BACKGROUND, NEXT_MEETING_BRIEF, WHO_AM_I_MEETING
  params[5]: intent?:string - Natural language request. Examples: "brief me for my 2pm with Alice", "prep me for tomorrow's board meeting".; calendarEventId?:string - Optional calendar event id to pull event details (title, time, location, attendees).; subject?:string - Subject line for the dossier (e.g. meeting title or topic).; attendeeHandles?:array of string - List of attendee handles/emails to look up in the relationships table.; generatedForAt?:string - ISO timestamp the dossier is generated for (defaults to now).
  example: User: "Pull up a dossier on Satya Nadella" -> actions: DOSSIER

  params[4]: subaction:string - One of: start, status, end, list.; intent?:string - Freeform owner intent / reason for the session. - CROSS_CHANNEL_SEND: Draft or send a message across any connected channel (email, telegram, discord, signal, sms, twilio_voice, imessage, whatsapp, notifications). Always drafts first; caller must re-invoke with confirmed: true to dispatch.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.
provider_hints[6]:
- images, attachments, or visual content -> ATTACHMENTS
- uploaded files, documents, or knowledge-base content -> AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS, KNOWLEDGE
- specific people or agents -> ENTITIES
- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

- actions execute in listed order

- STOP is a terminal control action even if it is not listed in available actions

- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.

# Providers
providers[15]:
- appBlocker: Admin-only provider for the native mobile app blocker integration (Family Controls on iPhone, Usage Access overlay on Android)
- ATTACHMENTS: Media attachments in the current message
- AVAILABLE_DOCUMENTS: List of documents available in the knowledge base. Shows which documents the agent can reference and retrieve information from.
- CURRENT_TIME: Provides current time and date information in various formats
- ENTITIES: Provides information about entities in the current context including users, agents, and participants
- FACTS: Provides known facts about entities learned through conversation
- KNOWLEDGE: Knowledge from the knowledge base that the agent knows, retrieved whenever the agent needs to answer a question about their expertise.
- RELATIONSHIPS: Relationships between entities observed by the agent including tags and metadata
- websiteBlocker: Admin-only provider for the local hosts-file website blocker integration. Use BLOCK_WEBSITES for timed or generic focus blocks, and BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE only when the unblock condition is finishing a task.
- WORLD: Provides information about the current world context including settings and members
- lifeops: Owner, explicitly granted users, and the agent only. Provides LifeOps overview plus live calendar and Gmail context. Route executable personal follow-through like todos, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and live todo-status questions to LIFE; live schedule and event requests to CALENDAR_ACTION; Gmail and email-specific work to GMAIL_ACTION; cross-channel inbox triage, digests, and reply workflows to INBOX; subscription audits, cancellations, and cancellation-status checks to SUBSCRIPTIONS; meeting-prep and person-background briefs to DOSSIER; X/Twitter reads and search to X_READ; fixed-duration or generic focus blocks to BLOCK_WEBSITES; task-gated focus blocks only to BLOCK_UNTIL_TASK_COMPLETE; stable owner profile or travel preferences only to UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE. Available in private owner or granted conversations, including Discord.
- activity-profile: Owner/admin and agent only. Compact user activity context: platform, time bucket, recency.
- lifeops_browser: Owner/admin-only context for the user's real Chrome and Safari browsers connected through LifeOps Browser. Separate from Milady Desktop Browser.
- inboxTriage: Injects pending inbox triage items into admin context. Shows urgent messages, items needing reply, and recent auto-replies across all channels including email. Use INBOX for cross-channel triage, digest, and respond workflows. Use GMAIL_ACTION instead when the request is explicitly Gmail-only, about unread emails, or about drafting/sending a specific email reply.
- crossChannelContext: Injects cross-channel hits (Gmail + agent memory + optional WS3 cluster fan-out) when the turn carries an explicit crossChannelContextRequest signal identifying a query or person. Admin/owner only. Silent on turns without signal.

- connections between people -> RELATIONSHIPS
- factual lookup -> FACTS
- world or environment context -> WORLD

- LIFE: Manage the user's personal routines, habits, goals, reminders, alarms, and escalation settings through LifeOps. USE this action for: creating, editing, or deleting tasks, habits, routines, and goals; todo and goal requests like 'add a todo: pick up dry cleaning tomorrow', 'remember to call mom on Sunday', 'what's on my todo list today?', or 'set a goal to save $5,000 by the end of the year'; setting one-off alarms or wake-up reminders like 'set an alarm for 7am' or 'wake me up at 7'; helping the user actually set up follow-through when they say things like 'help me brush my teeth every day', 'i keep forgetting x', or 'help me actually do it'; using LifeOps defaults for common routines when the user gives a natural window instead of an exact clock, like water reminders, stretch breaks, weekday-after-lunch Invisalign checks, twice-weekly shave reminders, or brushing when they wake up and before bed; marking items as complete, skipping, or snoozing them; reviewing goal progress; setting up phone/SMS escalation channels; adjusting reminder frequency or intensity; querying an overview of active LifeOps items. These are executable LifeOps items, not profile facts or bio updates. ALWAYS use LIFE for dynamic status questions like 'what's still left for today', 'what do i still need to do today', or 'anything else in my LifeOps list', even when the conversation already mentioned tasks, because their status may have changed after a completion, snooze, or reminder. Do not fall back to REPLY, UPDATE_ENTITY, or UPDATE_OWNER_PROFILE when the user is asking to create or inspect a todo, habit, goal, reminder, or alarm. DO NOT use this action for generic coaching or advice questions like 'any tips on setting better goals?' unless the user is also asking you to create, update, review, or track a concrete goal, task, reminder, or routine. DO NOT use this action for Gmail inbox triage, email search, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for daily briefs, unread summaries, drafts awaiting sign-off, or cross-channel inbox review — use INBOX, GMAIL_ACTION, or SEARCH_ACROSS_CHANNELS instead. DO NOT use this action for calendar lookups, scheduling meetings, searching events, or travel itineraries — use CALENDAR_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for multi-device push ladders or device-wide reminder delivery — use PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT instead. DO NOT use this action for pre-event asset checklists, document-signing workflows, collecting updated ID copies, or cancellation-fee warning/escalation policies — use INBOX, PUBLISH_DEVICE_INTENT, CALENDAR_ACTION, or LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. DO NOT use this action for browser/portal/file workflows on the owner's machine — use LIFEOPS_COMPUTER_USE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action or fall back to advice-only chat when the user wants real LifeOps follow-through.
  aliases[16]: MANAGE_LIFEOPS, QUERY_LIFEOPS, CREATE_TASK, CREATE_TODO, ADD_TODO, LIST_TODOS, TODO_LIST, CREATE_HABIT, CREATE_GOAL, LIFE_CREATE_DEFINITION, TRACK_HABIT, COMPLETE_TASK, SET_ALARM, SET_REMINDER, SNOOZE_REMINDER, SET_REMINDER_INTENSITY

Examples: "create a daily brushing habit for morning and night", "snooze brushing for 30 minutes", "what's on my calendar today".; title?:string - Name for a new item, or the name of an existing item to act on.; target?:string - Name or ID of an existing item when different from title (e.g., when renaming).; details?:object - Structured data when needed. - CALENDAR_ACTION: Interact with Google Calendar through LifeOps. USE this action for: viewing today's or this week's schedule; checking the next upcoming event; searching events by title, attendee, location, or date range; creating new calendar events; requests like 'what's my next meeting?', 'show me my calendar for today', 'what does my week look like?', or 'schedule a dentist appointment next Tuesday at 3pm'; querying travel itineraries, flights, hotel stays, trip windows, reserving recurring time blocks, and rebooking or moving calendar-backed commitments. These are live calendar reads and writes, so do not answer them from provider context alone and do not fall back to NONE or REPLY when this action is available. DO NOT use this action when the user is only making an observation like 'my calendar has been crazy this quarter' unless they actually ask you to inspect or change calendar state. DO NOT use this action for email inbox work, drafting or sending emails — use GMAIL_ACTION instead. DO NOT use this action for personal habits, goals, routines, or reminders — use LIFE instead. This action provides the final grounded reply; do not pair it with a speculative REPLY action.

  example: User: "What's on my calendar today?" -> actions: CALENDAR_ACTION

DO NOT use this action when the user is only complaining about email or messages without asking for triage, a digest, or a reply workflow.
